mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that outweigh corporate earnings resilience, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary suppression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is flat, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance with Rank IC below 0.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate wages, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, eroding real corporate margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with Rank IC below 0.01 during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production reflect synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing inventory bottlenecks and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and INDPRO uptick fails to produce positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance with Rank IC below 0.02 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of late-cycle observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.01 in this specific regime.
